pageTimings.text: Every morning, Glimmer practised. Fire breathing was the most important thing a dragon could do. And Glimmer could not do it yet.
On the first day: a small puff of smoke. Just smoke. No fire at all.
On the second day: a tiny spark. It landed on a leaf. It glowed for one second. Then it went out.
"Maybe you need to try harder," said Spark. Spark's own magic crackled around his ears. Glimmer tried harder. More smoke came out. Still no fire.
On the fifth day, Glimmer breathed in as deeply as possible. A warm puff came out. It smelled of cinnamon. "That's something," said Pip.
"That is not fire," said Glimmer. "No," agreed Pip. "But it smells nice." Glimmer did not look convinced.
On the tenth day, Glimmer went to the Crystal Stream alone. Again and again. Smoke. Sparks. Warm cinnamon breath. Still no fire.
Glimmer sat down. "I will never breathe fire," said Glimmer. "I have tried every day and it is never fire."
That evening, a Fog Marsh fox came creeping into Moonwood. It had found Fern's seed collection. The seeds were small and precious. Glimmer saw the fox.
Glimmer did not think. Glimmer just ran toward the fox. Glimmer breathed in as hard as possible.
And a jet of brilliant orange fire came out. The fox ran. The seeds were safe.
Glimmer stared at their own mouth. "Oh," said Glimmer. The fire had been inside all along. "I just needed to really mean it."
